seasonArc: Franziska Koch spent the 2026 cobbled spring as a luxury domestique for Demi Vollering at FDJ United-SUEZ — and then stole the biggest day of the campaign for herself. The German national champion's season turned at Paris-Roubaix Femmes on 12 April, where she was the only non-Visma rider to make the four-up move Pauline Ferrand-Prévot launched at Mons-en-Pévèle with 46 km to go. Left one-against-two once Blanka Vas was dropped, Koch refused to be controlled: strongest on the cobbles, repeatedly forcing Ferrand-Prévot to chase, then ice-cold in the velodrome where she held her line to beat Marianne Vos in a head-to-head sprint. It was the standout victory of her career and her first Monument, recasting a rider known for selfless work into a Classics winner in her own right — and capping a banner week for FDJ that also included the women's Tour of Flanders.
